metadata.name: Core Information System
metadata.description: Conceptual map of the information management system described in the ISO 19650 draft revision.
metadata.author: ISO19650 Knowledge Maps

nodes.label: Asset
nodes.description: Physical or built asset being managed.

nodes.label: Asset Lifecycle
nodes.description: Lifecycle stages of an asset.

nodes.label: Decision
nodes.description: Decision made during the lifecycle of an asset.

nodes.label: Information Purpose
nodes.description: Reason why information is required.

nodes.label: Information Requirement
nodes.description: Specification of required information.

nodes.label: Organizational Information Requirements
nodes.description: Information required to support organizational objectives.

nodes.label: Asset Information Requirements
nodes.description: Information required for asset management.

nodes.label: Project Information Requirements
nodes.description: Information required during project delivery.

nodes.label: Information Production Requirements
nodes.description: Information requirements relating to an appointment. (DEPRECATED: Exchange Information Requirements)

nodes.label: Information Production Team
nodes.description: Team responsible for producing information. (DEPRECATED: delivery team)

nodes.label: Information Manager
nodes.description: Role responsible for managing the information process.

nodes.label: Appointing Party
nodes.description: Organization appointing teams and defining requirements.

nodes.label: Lead Appointed Party
nodes.description: Organization responsible for managing information production.

nodes.label: Appointed Party
nodes.description: Organization responsible for generating information.

nodes.label: Information Container
nodes.description: Unit of information managed in the CDE.

nodes.label: Information Model
nodes.description: Structured collection of information containers.

nodes.label: Project Information Model
nodes.description: Information model used during delivery.

nodes.label: Asset Information Model
nodes.description: Information model used during asset operation.

nodes.label: Information Production
nodes.description: Activity of creating information, including generation, coordination, checking, reviewing, approval, authorization, acceptance and aggregation.

nodes.label: Information Verification
nodes.description: Checking information before exchange.

nodes.label: Information Exchange
nodes.description: Transfer of information between parties.

nodes.label: Information Use
nodes.description: Using information to support decisions.

nodes.label: Common Data Environment
nodes.description: Operational framework to facilitate the collaborative production of information, combining information production workflow and enabling technologies.

nodes.label: Information Management Process
nodes.description: Process governing information management throughout the asset lifecycle.

nodes.label: Information User
nodes.description: Person or organization using information.

nodes.label: Information Production Schedule
nodes.description: Details of information containers to be produced during an appointment. (DEPRECATED: task information delivery plan, master information delivery plan)

nodes.label: Information Production Standard
nodes.description: Set of rules for generating, coordinating and checking information containers.

nodes.label: Information Production Methods and Procedures
nodes.description: Library of available methods and procedures to support information production.

nodes.label: Trigger Event
nodes.description: Event that changes an asset, initiating a project.

nodes.label: Project
nodes.description: Information management and production response to a trigger event.

evidence.quote: Information management should be aligned with the life cycle of the asset(s) being managed by the appointing party.
Information is needed for information purposes and decision-making during all parts of the asset life cycle.
Information is only specified and produced in response to one or more clear information purposes.
Information requirements are generated from information purposes.
Information production requirements are derived from information requirements.
Information production includes generation, coordination, checking, reviewing, approval, authorization, acceptance and aggregation of information containers.
Information production includes... exchange, delivery, issue, dissemination, distribution...
Information containers are retrievable from within a file, system or application storage hierarchy.
An information model is a set of interrelated structured information containers.
Information models produced by information production teams contribute to the AIM in response to trigger events.
An asset information model (AIM) is the current virtual representation of a specific asset.
Aggregation of information to the asset information model should occur once information has been accepted by the appointing party.
Information models are used to facilitate decision making.
Information is needed for information purposes and decision-making during all parts of the asset life cycle.
Information production includes generation and aggregation of information containers.
Asset information requirements derive from organizational information requirements.
Project information requirements derive from asset information requirements.
